messages.invalid: Enter a valid contact email address, like ops@acmecatering.com - or leave it blank.
messages.hiddenCharacter: This address contains a hidden character - retype it, or leave it blank.
messages.tooLong: That email address is too long - use 256 characters or fewer, or leave it blank.

_comment: THE shared accept/reject corpus for the vendor contact-email predicate (#369).
Read by BOTH api/CompliDrop.Api.Tests/ContactEmailTests.cs and
frontend/src/lib/contact-email.test.ts, so a case added here is pinned on both sides and
the two implementations (api Services/ContactEmail.cs <-> frontend/src/lib/contact-email.ts)
cannot silently drift. Hand-maintained parallel lists were a review finding on the first
pass: they were already unequal at introduction.
It lives in the api test tree, NOT under docs/, so api-ci's 'api/**' filter covers a
corpus-only edit; frontend-ci names it explicitly. Under docs/ NEITHER workflow ran for the
one change most likely to break the guarantee this file provides (a second-pass finding).
Invisible characters are \uXXXX escapes ON PURPOSE - a literal one is unreviewable.
They exist because .NET's \s and JS's \s are DIFFERENT character classes (.NET's includes
U+0085 and excludes U+FEFF; JS's is the reverse), and Trim() vs .trim() diverge on the same
two code points. Both implementations therefore spell the blank class out explicitly instead
of relying on \s - these cases are what proves it. U+0000 additionally cannot be stored by
Postgres at all, so admitting it would 500 rather than 400.
COVERAGE RULE: every range component of the blank class - including BOTH endpoints of every
multi-code-point range - carries a 'malformed' case (mid-string, where stripping cannot hide
it) AND a 'paddedValid' case (at an edge, where only the strip set is exercised). Those are
two different code paths over the same set, so a range present in one and missing from the
other only surfaces if both are covered. 'valid' additionally pins the code points ADJACENT
to every range bound, so an off-by-one that widened a range is caught as a false rejection.
'maxLength' is the varchar(256) column width, asserted against each side's own constant so
the two cannot drift - it was previously the only rule of the pair this file did not pin.
'blankRanges' is the blank class ITSELF. The case lists above only SAMPLE it, so a range
added to one mirror alone used to pass both suites whenever the added code points were not
sampled. Each side now walks the BMP asserting its own predicate against these ranges, which
makes cross-language equivalence total. Adding a range means editing this list AND both
predicates - which is the point.
'messages' is the user-facing rejection copy, shared so the client's inline message and the
server's 400 body cannot diverge. Hyphens here are ASCII on purpose: this file is consumed
by two languages and an em-dash round-trips badly through some tooling. Each side asserts
its own constants against these strings.
'valid' means ONLY 'the predicate accepts this', not 'this is a good address'. Some adjacency
probes are bidi controls (U+202A, U+202E) - deliberately not in the blank class, so listed
here to pin the bound. Whether bidi controls belong in a stored address is a separate
question from #369 (this predicate is a typo catcher, not an RFC 5322 parser).
